agent_output: {"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Bird explicitly states 'Faceted filtering (by category, price range, brand) is explicitly out of scope for this release.' MJ's architecture includes faceted filtering by category (term filter), price_min/price_max (range filter), and brand (term filter). These directly contradict each other on whether faceted filtering should be built.","agents_involved":["Bird","MJ"],"options":["Option A: Follow Bird's scope ruling — remove all faceted filtering from MJ's architecture. SearchService accepts only a required keyword parameter and returns relevance-sorted results. Faceted filtering becomes a future release item.","Option B: Expand scope to include faceted filtering — Bird revises the scope note to include facets, and adds domain rules and acceptance criteria covering category, price range, and brand filters.","Option C: Build the Elasticsearch infrastructure to support facets internally but do not expose filter parameters in the API for this release, keeping the public contract aligned with Bird's scope while reducing future rework."],"recommendation":"Option A. Bird is the Domain Authority and Final Arbiter — the scope note is unambiguous ('explicitly out of scope for this release'). MJ's architecture should be revised to remove faceted filtering before handing off to Shaq. Implementing out-of-scope features wastes effort and risks shipping untested surface area with no acceptance criteria."}],"confidence":{"level":35,"high_confidence_areas":["Both agents agree on AND-logic keyword search","Both agents agree on relevance-score descending sort order","Elasticsearch as the backing engine is uncontested"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether faceted filtering should be included — this is the blocking contradiction","Whether MJ included facets intentionally (perhaps anticipating a future release) or mistakenly"],"assumptions":["Bird's scope note represents a deliberate product decision, not an oversight","MJ's inclusion of facets was a design choice, not a copy-paste artifact"]}}
